seoTitle: How to Mark a Bill as Paid in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Update a bill's status to paid in QuickBooks Online to keep expense tracking accurate and avoid late fees on vendor payments.
intro: Marking a bill as paid correctly in QuickBooks Online keeps your expense tracking accurate and helps avoid confusion around outstanding vendor balances. This guide walks through the process and how it fits into broader accounts payable management.

steps.heading: Navigate to your bills list
steps.body: Open the accounts payable section in QuickBooks Online where outstanding bills are listed.

steps.heading: Select the bill to update
steps.body: Find the specific bill that has been paid and open its details.

steps.heading: Record the payment
steps.body: Enter the payment details, including the amount, date, and payment method used to settle the bill.

steps.heading: Confirm the bill status updates
steps.body: Check that the bill now shows as paid and that the vendor's outstanding balance reflects the payment.

steps.heading: Review for accuracy
steps.body: Double-check the payment entry against your bank records to make sure everything matches up correctly.

faq.question: What happens if I mark a bill as paid by mistake?
faq.answer: You can typically go back into the transaction and correct or delete the payment entry to reflect the accurate status.

faq.question: Does marking a bill as paid automatically update my bank feed?
faq.answer: If the payment was made through a connected bank account, QuickBooks can match the transaction, but manual entries may need to be reconciled separately.

faq.question: Can I mark a bill as partially paid?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online supports partial payments, letting you record a portion of the bill as paid while the remainder stays outstanding.

faq.question: Why is accurate bill payment tracking important for cash flow?
faq.answer: Keeping bill statuses accurate helps you see a clear picture of upcoming obligations and avoid missed payments or late fees.
